Suppliers

A supplier is any organisation or individual that your business purchases from. Merlin keeps a supplier list so that invoices can be matched to the right contact, default accounts can be pre-filled, and payment terms can be applied automatically. Suppliers can be created manually or created automatically by the invoice processing pipeline when it encounters an unknown supplier name.

What is a supplier

In Merlin, a supplier is a party record (a business or person) that has a supplier relationship attached to it. The supplier relationship stores accounts payable defaults specific to that supplier. When an invoice arrives naming a supplier that does not yet exist in the system, the pipeline creates a new supplier record automatically with a pending status, ready for a staff member to review and approve.

Creating a supplier

  1. Go to Expenses › Suppliers.
  2. Click New Supplier.
  3. Fill in the supplier details in the form panel that opens on the right.
  4. Click Save.

Supplier fields

FieldRequiredNotes
Legal nameYesThe full registered name; max 255 characters
Trading nameNoDisplay name if different from legal name; max 255 characters
EmailNoMust be a valid email address; max 255 characters
PhoneNoMax 50 characters
NotesNoInternal notes; max 1 000 characters
StatusYesOne of active, pending, or inactive
Default payable accountNoThe accounts payable GL account used when processing this supplier's invoices; overrides the system default
Payment termNoThe default payment term applied to invoices from this supplier

Default payable account and payment term

The default payable account and payment term set on a supplier are stored on the supplier's relationship record (not on the supplier itself). When the invoice processing pipeline creates or matches an invoice to this supplier, it uses these defaults to pre-fill the payable account and calculate the due date. You can override both values on any individual invoice.

If no default is set on the supplier, the system-level default payable account code from Purchasing Settings is used instead.

Pending suppliers from the pipeline

When the invoice processing pipeline receives an invoice that names an unknown supplier, it automatically creates a new supplier record with the status pending. The pipeline attempts to match by tax number first, then by name similarity. If neither match is found, the new pending supplier is created using the name extracted from the invoice.

Pending suppliers appear in the suppliers list and must be reviewed by a staff member. To approve a pending supplier, open it from the list, verify the details are correct, change the status to active, and save. Until a supplier is approved, its invoices will remain in the review queue.

The supplier detail page

Clicking on a supplier's name in the list opens the supplier detail page at /suppliers/{id}. This is a read-only view showing the supplier's details, contact assignments, addresses, and recent documents. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact panels shown on this page.

Permissions

Creating and editing a supplier touches several models, so multiple permissions are involved:

  • parties-view-any / parties-view / parties-create / parties-update — to read and write the party record
  • party-relationships-create / party-relationships-update — to write the supplier relationship and its metadata
  • businesses-create / businesses-update or persons-create / persons-update — depending on whether the supplier is a business or a person

At minimum, a user needs create and update permissions across parties, party relationships, and businesses (or persons) to create and edit suppliers.
